message:
fix(abi): add missing errno propagation, fix termios speed storage, and harden search/pthread correctness

dirent_abi.rs:
- Set errno to EBADF on every early-return path in readdir() where the
  directory pointer is null, the registry is missing, or the handle is
  not found.  Previously these paths returned null without setting errno,
  leaving callers unable to distinguish "end of directory" from "bad fd".
- Set errno to EIO on the final fallthrough path in readdir() to signal
  an unexpected I/O condition rather than silently returning null.

pthread_abi.rs:
- When pthread_cancel() targets the calling thread and async cancellation
  is enabled, invoke pthread_testcancel() instead of merely consuming the
  pending flag.  This ensures the full POSIX cancellation cleanup-handler
  sequence executes (stack unwinding, cleanup pops, thread-exit) rather
  than silently swallowing the cancel request.

search_abi.rs:
- Add #[repr(C)] to HashSlot so that its field layout is guaranteed to
  match the C Entry struct.  The search() function casts *mut HashSlot to
  *mut Entry; without repr(C) the Rust compiler is free to reorder fields,
  which would cause key/data to appear at wrong offsets and corrupt
  results.

time_abi.rs:
- In time() and clock(), propagate the host errno via last_host_errno()
  on clock_gettime failure instead of silently returning -1.  This makes
  them consistent with gettimeofday(), which already used this pattern.
- In gettimeofday(), switch the existing set_abi_errno(EINVAL) call to
  last_host_errno(EINVAL) so the real host errno is preserved when
  available.

termios_abi.rs:
- Rewrite cfgetispeed() to read c_ispeed first and fall back to
  cfgetospeed() only when c_ispeed is zero, matching POSIX semantics
  where input and output speeds are independently stored.
- Rewrite cfgetospeed() to read c_ospeed first and fall back to the
  CBAUD mask in c_cflag only when c_ospeed is zero, preserving
  backwards compatibility with legacy termios structs.
- Change cfsetispeed() to write directly to c_ispeed instead of
  bit-packing into c_cflag, so input and output speeds are truly
  independent and do not clobber each other.
- Change cfsetospeed() to additionally write to c_ospeed alongside
  the existing c_cflag CBAUD update, keeping both representations
  in sync.

dirent_abi_test.rs:
- Add readdir_closed_dir_sets_ebadf: opens and immediately closes a
  directory, then verifies readdir() returns null AND sets errno to
  EBADF.
- Add readdir_r_closed_dir_reports_error: same scenario for readdir_r(),
  verifying the return code is EBADF and the result pointer is null.

termios_abi_test.rs:
- Update cfset_input_output_independent to verify that setting input
  speed to B9600 and output speed to B115200 preserves both values
  independently, asserting cfgetispeed returns B9600 (previously only
  output speed was checked).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
